## IVX IA Independence Layer — Complete

All 7 new files created, wired into `hono.ts`, TypeScript and Expo checks passing. Here's what was built:

### 1. Secure Variable Vault (`ivx-secure-vault.ts`)
9 IVX-prefixed variables: `IVX_GITHUB_TOKEN`, `IVX_RENDER_API_KEY`, `IVX_RENDER_SERVICE_ID`, `IVX_SUPABASE_URL`, `IVX_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`, `IVX_AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `IVX_AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`, `IVX_VERCEL_TOKEN`, `IVX_OWNER_TOKEN`. Each tested live, returns exact HTTP error on failure, falls back to Rork env vars gracefully. Never prints secrets.

### 2. IVX Tool Engine (`ivx-tool-engine.ts`)
16 unified tools: `github.read`, `github.write`, `github.commit`, `github.workflow`, `render.status`, `render.deploy`, `render.logs`, `render.rollback`, `supabase.audit`, `supabase.migrate`, `supabase.read_write_test`, `production.health`, `production.version`, `production.qa`, `commit.match`, `evidence.archive`. Every tool reads credentials from the vault, returns timed/secret-free results.

### 3. Senior Developer Brain (`ivx-senior-developer-brain.ts`)
8-phase lifecycle: **THINK → INSPECT → PLAN → EXECUTE → TEST → DEPLOY → VERIFY → REPORT**. Orchestrates the vault, tools, worker queue, and production evidence. Fast-fails with exact blocker when credentials are missing.

### 4. Self-Upgrade Engine (`ivx-self-upgrade-engine.ts`)
10 capabilities: build new modules, build apps, fix bugs, add APIs, add frontend screens, run QA, deploy, verify production, create rollback plan, improve own tools. Each reports READY/BLOCKED/FAILED with evidence.

### 5. Technology Scanner (`ivx-technology-scanner.ts`)
10 curated categories with 60+ tools: AI dev tools, deployment automation, GitHub automation, Render/Vercel/AWS, Supabase, AI coding agents, security, QA/testing, real estate AI, investor discovery. Built-in knowledge base for immediate results.

### 6. Independence Verifier (`ivx-independence-verifier.ts`)
Final test across 13 capabilities: GITHUB_WRITE, RENDER_DEPLOY, SUPABASE_WRITE, AWS_ACCESS, VERCEL_ACCESS, CHAT_COMMANDS, DEPLOY_COMMANDS, QA_COMMANDS, SELF_UPGRADE, EVIDENCE_ARCHIVE, 24_7_REPORT. FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED only with live evidence.

### 7. API Routes (`ivx-independence.ts`)
11 endpoints wired under `/api/ivx/independence/` — vault audit, tools catalog/execution, brain run/status, upgrade audit/run, scanner, verification, status.

### Credential Audit (current state)
| Variable | Source | Status |
|---|---|---|
| IVX_GITHUB_TOKEN | fallback → GITHUB_TOKEN | Present in `expo/.env` |
| IVX_RENDER_API_KEY | fallback → RENDER_API_KEY | Present in `expo/.env` |
| IVX_RENDER_SERVICE_ID | fallback → RENDER_SERVICE_ID | Present in `expo/.env` |
| IVX_SUPABASE_URL | fallback → SUPABASE_URL | Present in `expo/.env` |
| IVX_SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY | fallback | Present in `expo/.env` |
| IVX_VERCEL_TOKEN | none | Not configured |
| IVX_AWS_* | none | Not configured |

All credentials currently flow from the Rork fallbacks in `expo/.env` — when you set `IVX_` prefixed vars in Render's env vars or `.env`, the vault will prefer them automatically.

To push to production and verify end-to-end, trigger a Render deploy or push to GitHub.
